2604.07468.title: M-ArtAgent: Evidence-Based Multimodal Agent for Implicit Art Influence Discovery
2604.07468.paper_summary: Implicit artistic influence, although visually plausible, is often undocumented and thus poses a historically constrained attribution problem: resemblance is necessary but not sufficient evidence. Most prior systems reduce influence discovery to embedding similarity or label-driven graph completion, while recent multimodal large language models (LLMs) remain vulnerable to temporal inconsistency and unverified attributions. This paper introduces M-ArtAgent, an evidence-based multimodal agent that reframes implicit influence discovery as probabilistic adjudication. It follows a four-phase protocol consisting of Investigation, Corroboration, Falsification, and Verdict governed by a Reasoning and Acting (ReAct)-style controller that assembles verifiable evidence chains from images and biographies, enforces art-historical axioms, and subjects each hypothesis to adversarial falsification via a prompt-isolated critic. Two theory-grounded operators, StyleComparator for Wolfflin formal analysis and ConceptRetriever for ICONCLASS-based iconographic grounding, ensure that intermediate claims are formally auditable. On the balanced WikiArt Influence Benchmark-100 (WIB-100) of 100 artists and 2,000 directed pairs, M-ArtAgent achieves 83.7% positive-class F1, 0.666 Matthews correlation coefficient (MCC), and 0.910 area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (ROC-AUC), with leakage-control and robustness checks confirming that the gains persist when explicit influence phrases are masked. By coupling multimodal perception with domain-constrained falsification, M-ArtAgent demonstrates that implicit influence analysis benefits from historically grounded adjudication rather than pattern matching alone.
2604.07468.paper_summary_zh: 隱含的藝術影響，儘管在視覺上似乎合理，卻常常缺乏文獻記載，因此造成了一個歷史上受限的歸因問題：相似性是必要但不足的證據。大多數先前的系統將影響發現簡化為嵌入相似性或標籤驅動的圖形補全，而最近的多模態大型語言模型（LLMs）仍然容易受到時間不一致性和未經驗證的歸因影響。本文介紹了M-ArtAgent，一個基於證據的多模態代理，將隱含影響的發現重新框架為概率裁決。它遵循一個由調查、證實、反駁和裁決四個階段組成的協議，受一個推理與行動（ReAct）風格的控制器管理，該控制器從圖像和傳記中組裝可驗證的證據鏈，強制執行藝術史公理，並通過一個獨立的批評者對每個假設進行對抗性反駁。兩個基於理論的運算子，StyleComparator用於Wolfflin形式分析，ConceptRetriever用於基於ICONCLASS的圖像學基礎，確保中間聲明在形式上可審計。在平衡的WikiArt影響基準-100（WIB-100）中，包括100位藝術家和2,000對指向性配對，M-ArtAgent達成了83.7%的正類F1、0.666的馬修斯相關係數（MCC）和0.910的接收者操作特徵曲線下面積（ROC-AUC），並且泄漏控制和穩健性檢查確認當顯性影響短語被遮蔽時，增益仍然持續。通過將多模態感知與領域約束的反駁相結合，M-ArtAgent顯示出隱含影響分析受益於歷史上基礎的裁決，而不僅僅是模式匹配。
2604.07468.author: Hanyi Liu et.al.
2604.07468.authors: Hanyi Liu, Zhonghao Jiu, Minghao Wang, Yuhang Xie, Heran Yang
